differentials.name: protein-losing enteropathy with hypoalbuminemia ← loss of albumin into gastric lumen and increased loss of enteric protein + peripheral edema
differentials.raw: protein-losing enteropathy with hypoalbuminemia ← loss of albumin into gastric lumen and increased loss of enteric protein + peripheral edema

differentials.name: absent / decreased acid secretion
differentials.raw: absent / decreased acid secretion (> 50%) ← reduction in number of parietal + chief cells Location:   throughout fundus + body, particularly prominent along greater curvature; relative sparing of antrum (involved in 46%) (DDx to lymphoma: usually in antrum)

context: [Pierre Eugène Ménétrier (1859-1935), pathologist in Paris, France] =  GIANT HYPERTROPHIC GASTRITIS = HYPERPLASTIC GASTROPATHY =  rare disease characterized by excessive mucus production and TRIAD of   (1)  Giant mucosal hypertrophy + hypersecretion Prevalence:   < 1÷200,000 Cause:   ? overproduction of polypeptide growth factor α → increasing gastric mucus production + inhibiting gastric acid secretion Path:   mucosal thickness up to 6.0 mm (normal: 0.6-1.0 mm) Histo:   hyperplasia of glandular tissue + microcyst formation Age:   bimodal distribution in children < 10 years (← CMV infection) a
